top5Opportunities.statement: Minimize the elapsed time from profile geometry approval to first PPAP-qualified delivery of a new custom die cross-section geometry
Minimize the time to restore qualified precision profile supply after a SCAR and production containment event
Minimize the likelihood of selecting a profile supplier with insufficient process capability (Cpk < 1.67) requiring re-sourcing after PPAP tooling investment
Minimize the likelihood of dimensional non-conformance at first-article PPAP inspection requiring a die re-cut and full restart of the qualification cycle
Minimize the cost of Tier 1 production line disruption caused by a non-conforming profile delivery discovered at the machining cell

needs.statement: Minimize the elapsed time from profile geometry approval to first PPAP-qualified delivery of a new custom die cross-section geometry
needs.importanceRationale: 6-12 week tooling lead time is the single biggest timeline risk in automotive program launches using custom profiles. Each week of delay pushes the OEM SOP date.

needs.statement: Minimize the time to restore qualified precision profile supply after a dimensional non-conformance event triggers a Tier 1 SCAR and production containment
needs.importanceRationale: SCAR-triggered containment stops the Tier 1 production line until root cause is identified and corrective action verified. Recovery speed is the primary cost driver.

needs.statement: Minimize the likelihood of selecting a profile supplier with insufficient process capability (Cpk < 1.67) for the required tolerance class, requiring re-sourcing after PPAP tooling investment
needs.importanceRationale: Cpk < 1.67 disqualifies a supplier after tooling investment of 5-15K EUR has been committed. Re-sourcing adds 12-20 weeks to program timeline.

needs.statement: Minimize the likelihood of dimensional non-conformance discovered at first-article PPAP inspection requiring a die re-cut and full restart of the qualification cycle
needs.importanceRationale: Die re-cut adds 4-8 weeks to the qualification timeline plus additional material and engineering cost.

needs.statement: Minimize the cost of Tier 1 production line disruption caused by a non-conforming profile delivery discovered only at the machining cell — after incoming inspection release
needs.importanceRationale: Post-release detection means rework or scrap of partially machined parts plus line downtime cost.

needs.statement: Minimize the likelihood of a dimensionally non-conforming profile section being released to the Tier 1 machining cell undetected during incoming inspection
needs.importanceRationale: Non-conforming profiles that pass incoming inspection create downstream rework and potential OEM escalation.

needs.statement: Increase the ability to sustain on-time delivery of qualified precision profile stock across all call-off windows in a 52-week JIT program schedule without line-stoppage events
needs.importanceRationale: JIT delivery failure causes line stoppage at Tier 1 — direct cost plus OEM penalty.
